sources: Charaka Samhita (Kalpa Sthana, Siddhi Sthana, Chikitsa Sthana, Sutrasthana)
metadata.disclaimer: For AI-assisted educational recommendation only. Clinical application requires a qualified Vaidya (BAMS/MD Ayurveda).

vasanta.dosha_state: Kapha Prakopa — winter-accumulated Kapha is liquefied by spring warmth
vasanta.reason: Kapha is in Prakopa (aggravation) in Vasanta. Vamana expels liquefied Kapha from Amashaya (stomach). Classical: 'Vasante Kapha Kopah' (AH Sutrasthana 3.7)
vasanta.purvakarma_note: Snehana 3 days only (Kapha is already heavy). Bashpa Sweda before Vamana.

grishma.dosha_state: Pitta Sanchaya (accumulation) begins; Vata aggravated by summer dryness and heat
grishma.reason: Pitta begins accumulating but Bala (strength) is reduced in summer heat. Mild Raktamokshana for blood purification. Vigorous Shodhana contraindicated — use Shamana instead.
grishma.purvakarma_note: Only mild Snehana (Ghrita internally). Avoid vigorous Swedana in summer heat.

varsha.dosha_state: Vata Prakopa peak — cold, moist air, irregular food weakens Agni and aggravates Vata
varsha.reason: Basti is the supreme Vata therapy and Varsha is the peak Vata season. 'Varshashu Bastayah Prashastah' (CS Siddhi 1). Agni is weakest in monsoon — Vamana/Virechana risk Atiyoga.
varsha.purvakarma_note: Warm Abhyanga (Bala Taila or Dashamoola Taila) + gentle Nadi Sweda. Avoid exposure to rain/cold post-Swedana.

sharad.dosha_state: Pitta Prakopa peak — accumulated Pitta erupts in autumn heat. Rakta also vitiated.
sharad.reason: Sharad is the ideal Virechana season. Pitta is at peak aggravation. Virechana removes excess Pitta from Pakvashaya (colon) via Pachana Srotas. 'Sharade Pittam' (CS Sutrasthana 6.22)
sharad.purvakarma_note: Snehana 5 days with cooling ghee (Tikta Ghrita, Chandanadi Ghrita). Mild Swedana only — avoid overheating Pitta.

hemanta.dosha_state: Kapha Sanchaya begins; Agni is strongest in winter — body tolerates Shodhana well
hemanta.reason: Agni is at its strongest in Hemanta — ideal for Snehana and gentle Basti. Body has maximum tolerance for oleation.
hemanta.purvakarma_note: Intensive Snehana is well-tolerated. Abhyanga with Mahanarayana Taila or Bala Taila.

shishira.dosha_state: Vata Prakopa — cold, dry winds aggravate Vata; Kapha accumulates
shishira.reason: Basti for Vata; warming Nasya for Kapha in head. Raktamokshana contraindicated in cold — blood thickens and leech therapy is ineffective.
shishira.purvakarma_note: Heavy Snehana 7 days (Vata dominant season). Bashpa Sweda or Pinda Sweda to counter cold.

shodhana_eligibility.description: Classical assessment before deciding Shodhana (purification) vs Shamana (pacification). Ref: CS Sutrasthana 15, AH Chikitsa 1.
shodhana_candidate_criteria: Bala (strength): Medium to High — can withstand Shodhana stress
Agni: Sama or improving (Dipana-Pachana done if Manda Agni)
Ama: None or mild (Ama must be cleared by Deepana-Pachana first)
Kriya Kala: Prasara, Sthana, or Vyakti stage — disease has manifested enough for expulsion
Age: 7-70 years (outside this range — only Shamana)
shamana_only_criteria: Atidurbala (very weak/emaciated) — Shodhana would cause Dhatu Kshaya
Active fever (Jwara) — Shodhana during fever causes complications
Active diarrhea or dysentery — Virechana/Basti contraindicated
Severe bleeding disorders (Raktapitta) — except mild Raktamokshana for local conditions
High Ama without prior Deepana-Pachana — Shodhana drives Ama deeper into Srotas
Manda Agni without correction — purification cannot occur if Agni cannot process the dislodged doshas

ama_correction_first.duration_days: 3-7 days of Deepana-Pachana herbs before starting Snehana
ama_correction_first.diet_during: Light, warm, easily digestible food. Moong dal khichdi, soups. Avoid heavy, cold, raw, fermented.

purvakarma_protocols.description: Preparatory procedures mandatory before Pradhana Karma. Ref: CS Sutrasthana 22, AH Sutrasthana 2.

internal.name: Abhyantara Snehana (Internal Oleation)
internal.description: Progressive ghee/oil intake over 3-7 days to saturate tissues, loosen adhered Doshas from Srotas

vata.primary: Mahatiktaka Ghrita or plain Ghrita (cow's ghee)
vata.rationale: Ghrita builds Ojas, nourishes Asthi-Majja, lubricates dry Vata channels

pitta.primary: Tikta Ghrita (bitter ghee) or Shatavari Ghrita
pitta.rationale: Tikta Rasa ghee cools Pitta while oleating; avoids heating Pitta further

kapha.alternative: Sarshapa Taila (mustard oil) — warming, reduces Kapha
kapha.rationale: Kapha needs stimulating oleation — warming herbs prevent Kapha aggravation from heavy ghee

signs_adequate_snehana: Skin becomes soft and lustrous (Tvak Snigdha)
No hunger sensation at usual meal time
internal.diet_during_snehana: Only warm, light, easily digestible food. Rice gruel (Peya), moong dal soup. No cold, heavy, or dry food. No snacking.

external.name: Bahya Snehana (Abhyanga — External Oleation)
external.description: Full-body warm oil massage performed daily throughout Purvakarma

vata.primary: Sesame oil (Tila Taila) — warm, heavy, nourishing
vata.medicated: Bala Taila, Ksheerabala Taila, Mahanarayana Taila
vata.pressure: Medium to firm — Vata needs grounding

pitta.pressure: Light to medium — avoid excess heat

kapha.primary: Mustard oil (Sarshapa Taila) — light, penetrating, warming

external.technique: Always stroke towards the heart on limbs. Circular motion on joints. Clockwise on abdomen. Special focus on Marma points: Kshipra (between thumb and index), Talahridaya (palm/sole centre), Adhipati (crown of head).

swedana.name: Swedana (Sudation / Steam Therapy)
swedana.description: Applied after Abhyanga to open Srotas, increase circulation, and loosen adhered Doshas further. Ref: CS Sutrasthana 14.

bashpa_sweda.name: Bashpa Sweda (Full-body Steam)
bashpa_sweda.note: Avoid face in steam. Keep head cool with wet cloth.

nadi_sweda.name: Nadi Sweda (Localised Steam Pipe)
nadi_sweda.note: Applied to specific painful joints or Srotas. Common in Vata disorders.

pinda_sweda.name: Pinda Sweda (Navarakizhi / Bolus Fomentation)
pinda_sweda.materials: Navara rice cooked in milk + Dashamoola decoction, tied in cloth boluses (Kizhi), applied warm
pinda_sweda.note: Ideal for Vata neurological disorders, muscle wasting (Mamsa Kshaya), paralysis

home_steam.name: Home Steam Inhalation (Avagaha or towel steam)
home_steam.method: Boil water with Ajwain, Eucalyptus, or Dashamoola powder. Drape towel over head. Or use steam room/hot shower.

swedana.signs_adequate_swedana: Skin perspires uniformly. Body feels light and warm. Joints become supple. Doshas are ready for expulsion.

vamana.name: Vamana (Therapeutic Emesis)

pre_procedure: Day before: give Kapha-aggravating diet (curd, sesame, heavy sweets)
Morning of Vamana: apply Abhyanga on chest/back, give Bashpa Sweda
Have Vamana drugs ready and a trained Vaidya present
pradhana_procedure.aushadha_formula: Madanaphala Phanta: 1-2 Madanaphala fruits pounded, soaked in hot water, mixed with Yashtimadhu (liquorice) decoction, Pippali, Saindhava, Madhu (honey)
procedure_steps: Have patient drink 6-8 glasses of warm milk or sugarcane juice to fill stomach
Administer Vamana drug with Madhu and warm water
Patient sits on waist-height chair, supported by attendant
Mechanical stimulation of uvula if needed (with finger) to initiate reflex
Count Vega (episodes): note number and quality
After completion: wash mouth with warm water, rest lying on back
Dhumapana (medicated smoke inhalation) to clear Kapha from head

samyak_yoga_signs.vegashata: 8-12 episodes of emesis for adequate purification
samyak_yoga_signs.quality: Clear emesis at end (Kapha expelled completely)
samyak_yoga_signs.subjective: Patient feels light, clear mind, appetite returning, Kapha symptoms reduced

pradhana_procedure.atiyoga_signs: More than 12 episodes, bleeding in emesis, extreme weakness, fainting — STOP immediately, give Ghrita + warm milk
pradhana_procedure.ayoga_signs: Only 1-2 episodes, heaviness persists, Kapha not cleared — can repeat next day

indications: Kaphaja disorders: obesity, chronic respiratory (bronchitis, asthma), sinusitis, allergic rhinitis
Skin diseases (Kushtha) with Kapha involvement: psoriasis, eczema

relative: Vata-dominant Prakriti (use very mild dose, ensure Snehana first)

paschat_karma.immediate_rest: 24 hours complete rest. No talking unnecessarily. No exertion.
paschat_karma.dhumapana: Medicated smoke (Yasthimadhu, Haridra) for 30 minutes post-Vamana to clear remaining Kapha from head
pathya: Warm, light, easily digestible food throughout Samsarjana Krama
Avoid cold, heavy, oily food for 7 days
Avoid exertion, sex, long travel for 7 days

virechana.name: Virechana (Therapeutic Purgation)

pradhana_procedure.timing: Morning, 2-3 hours after sunrise, empty stomach, after completing Snehana + Swedana Purvakarma
pre_procedure: Last day of Snehana: light diet (rice, moong dal)
Morning of Virechana: mild Abhyanga on abdomen + Swedana

moderate.name: Eranda (Castor oil)
moderate.note: Ideal for Vata-Pitta. Most commonly used in current practice.

mild_home.name: Triphala Churna
mild_home.note: Very mild; home-safe; suitable for mild Virechana needs

pitta_specific.name: Avipattikara Churna
pitta_specific.note: Excellent for hyperacidity, GERD, Pitta disorders

procedure_steps: Administer drug after confirming Snehana is complete (signs of adequate Snehana present)
Patient lies down or sits quietly, avoid exertion
First purgation usually begins in 2-4 hours
Count Vegas (episodes) — record timing and nature
After purging: wash perineum with warm water, rest in supine position
Give warm water or Yashtimadhu decoction sips if thirsty

samyak_yoga_signs.vegashata: 20-30 episodes for full clinical Virechana; 5-10 for mild
samyak_yoga_signs.quality: Clear, non-mucoid stool at end — Pitta expelled
samyak_yoga_signs.subjective: Lightness, reduced burning sensations, mental clarity, improved appetite

pradhana_procedure.atiyoga_signs: More than 30 episodes, blood in stool, extreme weakness, rectal prolapse — STOP, give Yashtimadhu + Ghrita
pradhana_procedure.ayoga_signs: Fewer than 5 episodes, heaviness persists, Pitta symptoms unchanged

indications: All Pittaja disorders: acidity, GERD, gastritis, liver disorders
Skin diseases: psoriasis, eczema, acne, urticaria

paschat_karma.immediate_rest: Rest for remainder of day. Light walking acceptable after 2 hours.
pathya: Avoid cold, heavy, dry food for 5 days
Gentle lifestyle — no strenuous work for 3 days
apathya: Sour, pungent, salty taste excess (aggravates Pitta)

basti.name: Basti (Medicated Enema)
basti.note: Basti is the MOST IMPORTANT PK therapy — 'Ardha Chikitsa' (half of all treatment) for Vata. 'Basti is to Vata as Virechana is to Pitta.' (CS Siddhi 1)

yoga_basti.name: Yoga Basti (8-Basti Schedule)
yoga_basti.description: 8 Basties: 5 Anuvasana (oil) + 3 Niruha (decoction). Mild, suitable for most patients.

schedule.note: Start with oil — lubricates and prepares
End with oil — soothes and nourishes

yoga_basti.suitable_for: Most Vata conditions; home or clinic; mild to moderate disease

kala_basti.name: Kala Basti (16-Basti Schedule)
kala_basti.suitable_for: Moderate Vata disorders; chronic joint diseases; clinic setting

karma_basti.name: Karma Basti (30-Basti Schedule)
karma_basti.description: 30 Basties: 12 Niruha + 18 Anuvasana. Intensive Vata purification.
karma_basti.suitable_for: Severe chronic Vata disorders (hemiplegia, paraplegia, severe AS); clinic only

matra_basti.name: Matra Basti (Small Oil Enema — Home Adaptable)
matra_basti.description: 50-80ml warm medicated oil retained overnight. No expulsion forced. Daily or alternate days.
matra_basti.oil: Sesame oil, Bala Taila, or Dashamoola Taila
matra_basti.suitable_for: Home use. Vata conditions, dryness, constipation, lower back pain, insomnia.
matra_basti.note: Safest home Basti. No Vega (expulsion) needed — oil is absorbed.

niruha_basti.name: Niruha Basti (Decoction/Kashaya Basti — Shodhana)
niruha_basti.description: Combination of Kashayam (decoction), Taila (oil), Kalka (herbal paste), Madhu (honey), Saindhava (rock salt). Expels Vata from Pakvashaya.

classical_formula.preparation_note: Mix in sequence: Madhu → Saindhava → Sneha → Kalka → Kashayam. Never heat after mixing.

aushadha_by_condition.ankylosing_spondylitis: Dashamoola + Bala + Rasna Kashayam + Ksheerabala Taila

niruha_basti.procedure: Patient lies on left side. Nozzle (Netra) lubricated and inserted gently. Enema administered slowly in 1-2 minutes. Patient remains in left-side position 15-30 min. Expulsion occurs naturally within 30-60 minutes.
niruha_basti.samyak_yoga: Expulsion of flatus, stool, Kapha together. Patient feels lightness. Vata disorders improve.

anuvasana_basti.name: Anuvasana Basti (Oil/Sneha Basti — Brimhana)
anuvasana_basti.description: Small volume medicated oil enema. Retained overnight. Nourishes and lubricates.

oils_by_condition.reproductive: Shatavari Ghrita for female; Ashwagandha Ghrita for male

anuvasana_basti.retention: Retained until next morning or until urge to defecate

indications: All Vata disorders — primary treatment ('Ardhachikitsa' — half of all treatment)

relative: Inflammatory bowel disease in flare — use gentle Anuvasana only

paschat_karma.post_niruha_immediate: Rest 1-2 hours. Light warm meal after expulsion. Anuvasana Basti same evening.
paschat_karma.post_anuvasana_immediate: No specific action — oil retained and absorbed.
pathya: Continue light Vata-pacifying diet throughout Basti course
Avoid exertion after Niruha Basti day
Warm Abhyanga daily during Basti course

nasya.name: Nasya (Nasal Administration)
ideal_ritu: all — different types for different seasons
nasya.note: Nasa (nose) is the gateway to the head — Nasya reaches all organs of the head through Sringataka Marma. 'Nasam Hi Shiraso Dwaram' (CS Siddhi 9.89)

pratimarsha_nasya.name: Pratimarsha Nasya (Daily Preventive — Home)
pratimarsha_nasya.description: 2 drops of medicated oil in each nostril daily. Safe for everyone. Preventive and mild therapeutic.
pratimarsha_nasya.timing: Morning after brushing, before sunrise ideally

oils.vata: Anu Taila or plain Sesame oil — lubricates and warms
oils.pitta: Brahmi Ghrita or Ghrita (plain cow's ghee) — cooling
oils.kapha: Shadbindu Taila or Vacha Taila — stimulating, clears Kapha

pratimarsha_nasya.procedure: Tilt head back slightly. Administer 2 drops. Sniff gently. Massage nasal bridge. Remain supine 1-2 minutes. Do not eat immediately after.

navana_nasya.name: Navana Nasya (Oily Drops — Therapeutic)
navana_nasya.description: 6-8 drops medicated oil or ghee in each nostril. Therapeutic for head disorders.
navana_nasya.timing: Morning, 30 minutes after local Abhyanga + steam on face/neck

primary_aushadha.vata_head_disorders: Ksheerabala Taila 101 — for migraine, facial palsy, Vata in head
primary_aushadha.kapha_disorders: Shadbindu Taila — sinusitis, allergic rhinitis, nasal polyps
primary_aushadha.pitta_disorders: Brahmi Ghrita or Shatavari Ghrita — cooling, for inflammatory head disorders
primary_aushadha.neurological: Brahmi Taila or Maha Panchagavya Ghrita — memory, mental clarity
primary_aushadha.general: Anu Taila — universal, well-tolerated, preventive + mild therapeutic

virechana_nasya.name: Virechana Nasya (Purgative Nasya — Shodhana)
virechana_nasya.description: Pungent powders or liquids to expel Kapha from head and sinuses. Strong — clinic only.
virechana_nasya.dose: Medicated powder or 4 drops strong decoction
virechana_nasya.primary_aushadha: Apamarga (Achyranthes aspera) powder, Vacha powder, Pippali powder

shiro_basti.name: Shiro Basti (Oil Retention on Head)
shiro_basti.description: Warm medicated oil retained on scalp in a leather cap for 15-45 minutes. Deep Vata-Pitta therapy.
shiro_basti.oils: Ksheerabala Taila 101, Brahmi Taila, or plain Sesame oil

shirodhara.name: Shirodhara (Continuous Oil Stream on Forehead)
shirodhara.description: Continuous stream of warm oil on Ajna Chakra (third eye/Sthapani Marma) for 30-45 minutes. Profound psycho-neurological effect.

oils.vata: Tila Taila or Ksheerabala Taila — warm, grounding
oils.pitta: Chandanadi Taila or Brahmi Coconut oil — cooling
oils.kapha: Karpasasthyadi Taila — stimulating, clears mental Kapha

general_contraindications: Acute cough or cold (wait for it to resolve first)
Immediately after bathing in cold water
During menstruation (Pratimarsha is fine; Virechana Nasya — avoid)
Severe headache from Pitta (avoid stimulating Nasya)

raktamokshana.name: Raktamokshana (Blood Purification Therapy)
raktamokshana.note: Raktamokshana is the 5th Panchakarma. Modern practice primarily uses Jalaukavacharana (leech therapy) and Shringa (cupping). Siravyadha (venesection) is rarely used in modern settings.

jalaukavacharana.name: Jalaukavacharana (Leech Therapy)
jalaukavacharana.description: Medicinal leeches (Hirudo medicinalis) applied to affected area for blood purification

shringa.name: Shringa / Cupping (Horn/Cup Therapy)
shringa.description: Traditional or modern cups applied to create suction, drawing Rakta to surface. Home-adaptable with modern cups.
shringa.setting: home with dry cupping; clinic for wet cupping

pracchana.name: Pracchana (Scarification)
pracchana.description: Multiple small incisions on affected area to release vitiated Rakta

indications: All Rakta-Pitta disorders: acne, psoriasis, eczema, urticaria

samsarjana_krama.description: Systematic dietary re-introduction after Pradhana Karma. Critical to prevent Dhatu Kshaya post-Shodhana. Ref: CS Siddhi 1, AH Chikitsa 1.
samsarjana_krama.note: Duration and stages depend on the strength (Bala) of Shodhana: Pravara (strong) = more stages, Madhyama = medium, Avara (weak) = fewer stages.

post_vamana.description: After Vamana — re-entry over 7 days (Pravara Shuddhi)

stages.recipe: 1 part rice : 14 parts water, boiled. Strain and drink warm.
stages.note: Agni is lowest — only liquid rice water

stages.recipe: Moong dal boiled to soup. No salt, no spices, no oil.
Moong dal with mild spices: cumin, coriander, a pinch of saindhava salt, ghee.

stages.food: Akrita Mamsa Rasa / Shakas (plain vegetables or lentil broth)
stages.recipe: Plain boiled soft vegetables or lentil broth without heavy spices.

stages.food: Krita Mamsa / Krita Shakas (normal light cooked food)
stages.recipe: Khichdi (rice + moong dal), soft cooked vegetables, ghee.
Regular Ayurvedic diet appropriate for Prakriti/Vikriti.

post_virechana.description: After Virechana — re-entry over 5-7 days depending on strength

stages.recipe: Puffed/parched rice soaked in warm water. Strain. Add saindhava.
stages.note: Coolant; replaces fluids lost in purgation

stages.recipe: Moong dal with ghee, cumin, coriander, saindhava.
Equal rice + moong dal, well-cooked, with ghee and mild spices.
Avoid sour, pungent, oily, heavy food. Continue Pitta-pacifying diet for 30 days.

post_basti.description: After each Niruha Basti day — light diet. After full Basti course — 3-day re-entry.
post_basti.during_basti_course: Warm, light, easily digestible food on all Basti days. Khichdi, moong dal, warm soups. No cold, heavy, raw, dry food. No carbonated drinks.

rasayana_integration.description: After Shodhana, Dhatus are clean and receptive — ideal time for Rasayana (rejuvenation). Rasayana absorption is maximum post-Shodhana.
rasayana_integration.timing: Begin Rasayana on day 3-5 after Pradhana Karma

general_immunity.dose: 1 tsp twice daily with warm milk

reproductive_male.dose: 5g each with warm milk at night

bone_joint.dose: 2 tablets twice daily with warm water

dosha_pradhana_karma_mapping.description: Classical mapping of dominant dosha to primary Pradhana Karma. Ref: CS Sutrasthana 15, AH Sutrasthana 14.

vata.reason: Basti directly reaches Pakvashaya — the seat of Vata. Niruha expels; Anuvasana nourishes. Basti is 'Ardhachikitsa' for Vata.
vata.clinical_note: Never do Virechana alone for Vata — it further dries. Always follow with Basti.

pitta.reason: Virechana expels Pitta from Pakvashaya through its natural downward channel (Adho Marga). Raktamokshana for blood-level Pitta vitiation.
pitta.clinical_note: Virechana before Basti if both Vata and Pitta are high.

kapha.reason: Vamana expels Kapha from Amashaya (stomach) — the seat of Kapha. Nasya clears Kapha from Urdhvanga (head/neck).
kapha.clinical_note: Udvartana (dry powder massage) as Purvakarma instead of heavy Snehana — Kapha doesn't need excessive oil.

vata_pitta.reason: Address Pitta first (Virechana), then Vata (Basti). Treating Vata first with Basti while Pitta is high may aggravate.

pitta_kapha.reason: Virechana addresses both Pitta and reduces Kapha from below. Vamana for residual Kapha if needed.
pitta_kapha.sequence: Vamana → Virechana (or Virechana alone for mild cases)

vata_kapha.reason: Basti for Vata. Vamana for Kapha. Sequence depends on which is more aggravated.

sama.reason: Virechana is the safest universal Shodhana for Sama Prakriti. Follow with Yoga Basti for comprehensive treatment.

home_panchakarma_protocol.description: A safe, effective home Panchakarma protocol for users without clinic access. Based on Shamana and mild Shodhana principles.
home_panchakarma_protocol.disclaimer: Home protocol is Shamana (palliative), not full Shodhana. For Shodhana (Vamana, clinical Virechana, Niruha Basti), consult a Vaidya.

days_1_3.name: Purvakarma (Preparation)
days_1_3.morning: Deepana-Pachana herbs on empty stomach (Trikatu Churna 1g + warm water OR Hingashtak Churna 2g)

self_abhyanga.oil_vata: Warm sesame oil — 45 minutes full body
self_abhyanga.oil_kapha: Warm mustard oil — brisk stimulating massage

days_1_3.steam: Hot shower or steam room for 10 minutes after Abhyanga
days_1_3.diet: Light, warm, freshly cooked food only. No cold, heavy, processed food.

days_4_5.name: Mild Pradhana (Internal Oleation + Mild Purgation)
days_4_5.morning: Internal Snehana — Ghrita 30ml with warm water on empty stomach (skip if Kapha dominant — use Sarshapa Taila 15ml instead)
days_4_5.evening_day4: Triphala Churna 5g with warm water (mild laxative effect overnight)
days_4_5.evening_day5: Castor oil 15-20ml with warm milk at night (stronger purgation if needed — optional, skip if loose stools already)

home_basti.option: Matra Basti — 50ml warm sesame oil at night via enema bulb. Retain as long as possible.
home_basti.suitable_for: Vata conditions, constipation, lower back pain
home_basti.note: Home Matra Basti is safe. Avoid if active diarrhea or rectal issues.

days_4_5.nasya: Pratimarsha Nasya — 2 drops Anu Taila or Sesame oil per nostril in morning
days_4_5.diet: Kitchari (moong dal + rice) mono-diet. Nothing else. Warm ginger tea.

days_6_7.name: Paschat Karma (Recovery)
days_6_7.diet: Samsarjana Krama — begin with Peya, progress to normal light food
days_6_7.rasayana: Begin Chyawanprash 1 tsp twice daily or appropriate Rasayana
days_6_7.gentle_yoga: Gentle Pavanamuktasana series, Savasana, Yoga Nidra

21_day_intensive_home.note: For users with 21 days and strict adherence. Combines all elements with Kitchari mono-diet for 7 days.

structure.days_1_7: Purvakarma — daily Abhyanga, progressive internal Snehana (ghee), diet cleansing
structure.days_8_14: Pradhana phase — mild Virechana (Castor oil), daily Matra Basti, Pratimarsha Nasya, Kitchari mono-diet
structure.days_15_21: Paschat Karma — Samsarjana Krama dietary re-entry, Rasayana herbs, gentle Yoga/Pranayama, gradual return to normal life

aushadha_compendium.description: Classical formulations used in Panchakarma. For reference and LLM recommendation grounding.

oils_external.name: Tila Taila (Sesame oil)
oils_external.use: Universal Abhyanga base for Vata. Brimhana, warming.

oils_external.name: Ksheerabala Taila
oils_external.use: Neurological disorders, Asthi-Majja Vata, Basti for joint/neurological

oils_external.name: Mahanarayana Taila
oils_external.use: Joint disorders, muscle disorders, Kati Basti, Greeva Basti

oils_external.name: Bala Taila
oils_external.use: General Vata, muscle strength, Brimhana Basti

oils_external.name: Chandanadi Taila
oils_external.use: Cooling Abhyanga for Pitta, skin inflammation

oils_external.name: Brahmi Taila
oils_external.use: Shirodhara, Shiro Basti — neurological, mental health

oils_external.name: Dhanvantara Taila
oils_external.use: Postnatal care, Vata disorders, Greeva Basti

oils_external.name: Anu Taila
oils_external.use: Universal Navana Nasya. Most commonly used nasal oil.

ghrita_internal.name: Tikta Ghrita
ghrita_internal.use: Internal Snehana for Pitta. Virechana preparation.

ghrita_internal.name: Mahatiktaka Ghrita
ghrita_internal.use: Skin diseases, liver disorders, Pitta-Rakta. Virechana preparation.

ghrita_internal.name: Brahmi Ghrita
ghrita_internal.use: Medhya Rasayana, mental health, Nasya, Shirodhara

kashayam_basti.name: Dashamoola Kashayam
kashayam_basti.use: Most commonly used Niruha Basti base. Universal Vata.

kashayam_basti.name: Rasna Saptak Kashayam
kashayam_basti.use: Joint disorders, AS, RA — Niruha Basti

kashayam_basti.name: Triphala Kashayam
